headline: Sabah Railway Revival Plan Estimated at RM18 Billion
context: Federal government announced feasibility study in January 2026 for reviving and expanding Sabah colonial-era railway into modern network.

cards.big: RM18 billion feasibility study launched for Sabah railway modernization
cards.sub: Plan would convert 134km colonial track and add 280km new commuter lines

cards.big: Current Sabah State Railway carries only 1.2 million passengers annually
cards.sub: The 134km Tanjung Aru to Tenom line operates diesel trains at 40km/h

cards.big: Proposed network would connect KK Sandakan and Tawau with electrified rail
cards.sub: Journey time KK to Sandakan would drop from 6 hours road to 2.5 hours

cards.big: Previous railway proposals in 2008 and 2015 shelved due to cost concerns
cards.sub: Current plan relies on 60% federal funding and 40% private participation

cards.big: Railway revival tests whether Sabah infrastructure ambition gets beyond the study phase
RM18 billion for Sabah railway — the business case depends on freight volumes, not political promises.
